Records.authors: Bern, Zvi, Luna, Andres, Roiban, Radu, Shen, Chia-Hsien, Zeng, Mao
Records.description: One-loop scalar box integral I_B for 2-to-2 scattering of two distinct massive scalars (matter lines of mass m1 and m2) exchanging gravitons in the t-channel. Two opposite edges are massless (graviton exchanges) and two opposite edges are massive matter propagators of masses m1 and m2. Used as one of the basis integrals (with crossed-box, triangle, and triangle-down) in the reduction of the one-loop four-point amplitude in Eq. (Org_Scalar_Integrals). Result is taken from the literature (Beenakker-Denner) and expanded in the classical (small-q) limit; the box is more singular than classical so subleading terms in the polarization-to-spin map must be retained. The relevant nonrelativistic 3D form used in the EFT matching is given near Eq. (4.49).
Records.location: Sec. 4 (one-loop amplitudes), Eq. (4.??), Fig. OneLoopIntegralsFigure (a); explicit nonrelativistic form Eq. near (4.49)-(4.51)

Records.authors: Bern, Zvi, Luna, Andres, Roiban, Radu, Shen, Chia-Hsien, Zeng, Mao
Records.description: One-loop scalar crossed (u-channel) box integral I_Bbar for 2-to-2 scattering of two massive scalars of masses m1 and m2 exchanging gravitons. Obtained from I_B by crossing p2 <-> p3. Together with I_B, I_triangle, and I_triangledown forms the basis of scalar one-loop integrals appearing in the classical four-point amplitude in Eq. (Org_Scalar_Integrals). Evaluated using the result of Beenakker-Denner and expanded in the classical limit.

Records.authors: Parra-Martinez, Julio, Ruf, Michael S., Zeng, Mao
Records.description: One-loop scalar box integral with two massive matter lines and two massless graviton lines (graviton-dominance one-loop topology in the soft expansion). Generic kinematics, on-shell external legs p1^2=p4^2=m1^2, p2^2=p3^2=m2^2, t=q^2. Three master integrals after soft expansion (linearized matter propagators), reduced to a canonical pure basis (f1: bubble, f2: triangle, f3: box) and integrated to all orders in velocity in the potential region. Symbol alphabet contains the single letter x with y=(x+1/x)/2 the relativistic factor. Crossed box xII obtained by u2 -> -u2.
